REPLY TO PLTF'S OPPOS. TO DEMURRER AND MTN TO STRIKE FILED BY DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THIS MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND, FIFTH, SIXTH, NINTH AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, IT CANNOT BE ASCERTAINED WHETHER THE CONTRACT IS WRITTEN, ORAL OR IMPLIED BY CONDUCT. THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. NONE OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE UNCERTAIN. ORDER SIGNED. (302/HEK) JUDGE HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THIS MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. IT IS IMPROPER TO MOVE TO STRIKE AN ENTIRE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT. MOREOVER, WHILE THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION MAY NOT BE AN INDEPENDENT CAUSE OF ACTION, IT SEEKS RELIEF TAT IS AVAILABLE UNDER OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS NOT IMPROPER. SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL'S STATUS AS A RELIGIOUS CORPORATION DOES NOT APPEAR ON THE FACE OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS ADEQUATELY PLED. ORDER SIGNED. (302/HEK) JUDGE HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JULY 5, 2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO PROVIDE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFF MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPT. 302 NO LATER THAN 4PM JUNE 27 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. FAILURE TO DO SO WILL RESULT IN GRANTING THE MOTION. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976
2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF PIRIYEVA, LARISA AS TO DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL DOES 1-20

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: NO ARGUMENT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE CONTRACT MAY BE AMBIGUOUS AND THUS SUBJECT TO EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE. THE DEMURRER TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED: WHILE THE LANGUAGE IN THE PURPORTED CLAIM IS NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION AS SUCH, IT SEEKS RELIEF THAT PLAINTIFF'S OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION MAY SUPPORT, AND NO USEFUL PURPOSE WOULD BE SERVED BY STRIKING IT. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A MOTION TO AMEND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 425.14. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE APPEARS UNOPPOSED AND IS THUS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CURTIS E. A. KARNOW, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: GEORGE KHOURY, ESQ., LAW OFFICE OF GEORGE KHOURY, 415.771.6174, FOR PLAINTIFF LARISA PIRIYEVA.
KATHLEEN DUGGAN, ESQ., ROPERS MAJESKI KOHN BENTLEY, 650.364.8200, FOR DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. The contract may be ambiguous and thus subject to extrinsic evidence. The demurrer to the eighth cause of action is overruled: while the language in the purported claim is not a cause of action as such, it seeks relief that plaintiff's other causes of action may support, and no useful purpose would be served by striking it. The Court grants DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Plaintiff may file a motion to amend in compliance with CCP 425.14. The request for judicial notice appears unopposed and is thus granted. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Kathleen Duggan of Ropers Majeski Kohn Bentley (543.4800) for defendant and moving party; George S. Khoury of the Law Office of George S. Khoury (771.6174) for plaintiff and responding party.
This matter was not argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the first cause of action and overruled as to the second, fifth, sixth, ninth and tenth causes of action. As to the first cause of action, it cannot be ascertained whether the contract is written, oral or implied by conduct. The other causes of action are adequately pled. None of the causes of action are uncertain. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge Harold Kahn; Not reported

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-05-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Kathleen Duggan of Ropers Majeski Kohn Bentley (543.4800) for defendant and moving party; George S. Khoury of the Law Office of George S. Khoury (771.6174) for plaintiff and responding party.
This matter was not argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. It is improper to move to strike an entire cause of action for failure to state facts sufficient. Moreover, while the eighth cause of action may not be an independent cause of action, it seeks relief tat is available under other causes of action. The prayer for punitive damages is not improper. Sinai Memorial Chapel's status as a religious corporation does not appear on the face of the First Amended Complaint and the prayer for punitive damages is adequately pled. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge Harold Kahn; Not reported
